reviews.review_text: sexy stripper cop man tasering my nonexistent balls 10/10 experience

I honestly think The Outlast Trials is worth buying if you’re into horror games. The atmosphere is really creepy and the game actually keeps you tense the whole time instead of relying on cheap jump scares. It’s way more fun with friends too because everyone ends up panicking and screaming at each other during the trials. The graphics and sound design are really good and make everything feel intense. I also like that the game doesn’t get boring fast since every match feels a little different. Even when you know what’s coming, it still manages to stress you out. Overall I’d recommend it because it’s fun, scary, and actually replayable compared to a lot of other horror games.

I smashed my laptop after starting my first trial. 

After I sat down in that chair and watched that introduction video, I saw the character SGT Leland Coyle, and My erection got too hard, too fast, and it sent my PC flying into the ceiling, destroying my PC. Fix your game.

My favorite survival co-op horror game to this day. 

It's super fun, it's intense, and it gets amplified if you have friends to play with. I play this game a lot, and I mean, A LOT than what I expected.

So many trials to play and pick, there are game modes and difficulties you can pick that suits your playstyle. Whether you like playing trials, the current event running, a PvP mode called Invasion (don't like this one personally myself so far but some people do. More powers to them), or an endless roguelike mode (Escalation) if you feel like you can tackle the game at its hardest.

Another thing that I love about this game is that; they are super consistent to the lore which is surprising to me. Considering the game is a live service game, you'd think some lore or evidence files will contradict each other but no; as the game goes on and new modes or variators are added, they add lore that explains why that happens. These lore also foreshadows incoming variators, prime assets, or dangers by reading them, so they also act as a sneak peek in game. My favorite one is when Red Barrels closed the Rebirth mechanic for a while so they can rework it to be much better, but they added a lore in game saying that Dr. Easterman is disappointed in us for trying to escape (Amelia's Escape Incident), he blocked off rebirths until further notice.

Content is a bit slow, but they are consistent to it. It is a live service game, and for each major update they either add 2 MKs or with a full trial, if it is a new season. I do know that this game is not meant to be played daily and it is meant to bring you back overtime as the game receives updates. Because if you do play daily, then you will get burnt out immediately, especially if you have done everything in this game. I wish we could get more content, 3 MKs or even more. Kinda asking for too much from the poor devs but it shows how much I love this game. That would satisfy my insatiable hunger for content.

They do have premium currency and season content are locked behind it, but if you do pay for one season, the season's catalogue will pay for the rest of the seasons; assuming you did not spend those coins anywhere else. The rest of the event catalogues must be paid with currency if you want the cosmetics in them (they used to be missed content, but they brought them back for the people who missed the events).

All in all, fantastic game, pick it up if you love horror games, and if you have friends, even more of an incentive to pick it up and play with. If you feel the price is too much, the game goes on sale a bunch of times, and sometimes before or after a major update. So you can wait for it and buy it when it goes on sale.

Honestly didn’t expect The Outlast Trials to be this good. The atmosphere is incredible, the art direction is disturbing in the best way and the sound design makes everything feel way more immersive. Even after 39 hours, the trials still feel unpredictable which is rare for any co-op horror game.

One thing that really frustrated us though was the matchmaking and balance at higher levels. Me and my two friends were around level 85, 87, and 67 with no relapses, yet we were getting matched with players who had extremely high relapse counts, even up to 99. The skill gap was obvious, those players knew every shortcut and mechanic while we were still learning.

Definitely worth the play though, especially if you get it on sale.

While this game is still a fun experience, was it really necessary to not only use AI, but LIE about it, deceive consumers, and only then be forced by Steam to include that you have in fact been using Generative AI to fill in for artwork and posters?

Even if they were, it still wouldn't ever justify the usage of it but Red Barrels is no small company.

Finding this out was an absolute disappointment. The same money they probably spent and wasted on unmeaningful filler, they could've probably given to an actual artist. Shame I spent money on this.

A short rant/review of Outlast Trials.

Gameplay-wise Outlast Trials is either pretty good or just okay.

Missions are pretty fun on your first go around and there is much fun to be had in replaying them on harder difficulties or with different modifiers. Escalation is fun, probably the most fun I had because it quickly turns from easy pickings to ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ from hell, and the chaotic nature of it with friends is some of the most fun I've had playing this game. A lot of character designs are pretty good, with prime assets stealing the show obviously. I think their personalities and interactions are some of the coolest things I've ever seen in a game like this (of which I have not played many).

On the other hand...

The game can feel janky a lot of the time, whether it be netcode or simply the game not doing what you ask (ie closing a door right in front of yourself immediately after opening). Updates feel like 100% polyester clothing, that is to say cheap. Red Barrel seems to focus more on churning out updates for cosmetics than making the greatest update that they could make, which results in the previously mentioned jank persisting. 

As you play, it'll start as small, occasional annoyances, maybe you have some trouble moving through an area due to weird behaviors from door or enemies, but as you gain more experience and run higher difficulties you may begin to become frustrated with the game as a whole, as these previous annoyances can be death sentences in later challenge runs. The amount of times in escalation where Franco will shoot you with a ♥♥♥♥ you shrapnel from across the map on a mode with deadly prime asset, killing your ass faster than you can start a mission may be more than you'd wish.

Art/Story-wise Outlast Trials is almost the textbook definition of narrative dissonance.

For a game to so naturally weave in mind-numbing but enjoyable gameplay that is in line with the story it wishes to tell, but then turn around and spit in your face with A.I. content causes so much whiplash I could almost feel my head snap.

I think regardless of what you think of A.I. as a development or art tool, there is much to be said about the quality of this game throughout its lifetime. I won't lie and say I've been here since the beginning, but the lack of focus on refining core gameplay in the past almost year says everything that needs to be said about my thoughts about the future of this game. 

There is care and love put into aspects of the game, not everything about it is devoid of human choice and intent when it comes to its direction, there are people who care about this game and it's development on the team, but the inclusion of A.I. assets in this game is jarring.

If you advocate for this behavior in your games, with the high amount of A.I. content within this game specifically, you must acknowledge that you are advocating for paying for less. You want lower quality art, voice acting, and updates to feed you constant gameplay, quality be damned. 

Red Barrel isn't a small scale indie company, it can afford to pay people for the jobs that need to be done, but instead what we get is a Outlast-themed attempt at a forever game with A.I. generated stuff in a game with paid microtransactions.

In summary: I do have fun playing this game, but I don't believe that the way this game will continue is a game I want to continue playing for much longer.

I really enjoyed this game when I first started. Unfortunately I won't change this review until all the AI generated slop assets are removed. AI is the falloff of creativity and is giving into the end of real artists and capitalism, which they claim to be against. I thought this game was cool but the second I started noticing the obvious AI, I realized the devs simply don't care about artistic integrity. What even is the point in making these games if you have no motive or reason to convey a real authentic story? Oh wait. Money. Ironic.

This game is great, I love the gameplay and it's definitely scary, however as an artist it's really frustrating seeing such a big studio use AI to "enhance" their art direction. It just makes everything besides the main areas feel really cheap, the small details in games aren't necessarily meant to be skipped over, they're meant to bring together the environment as a whole. Those who are artists, game designers or just fans of the game can look at these small details and appreciate them. Instead, these are designed to pretty much be ignored. I'd love to sit and admire the level design but the overuse of AI really makes me not appreciate it as much as I could be.

I love this game, however, I would like to play it with one of my friends but it doesn't have any accessibility settings with flashing lights. It would be so awesome if there was a way to turn down or off some of the flashing lights

I’ve been a massive fan of the first two games, they’re legendary...so I was a bit skeptical about how this would work, but man, Outlast Trials is genuinely amazing. If you’re playing with friends, it’s a whole different level of fun because you’re all just panicking and laughing at the same time. The tasks they make you do are completely unhinged and honestly pretty messed up, but there’s this weirdly comical side to how horrifying it all is while you’re scrambling to survive.

The funniest and most stressful part is when a simple 15 minute objective ends up taking over an hour because everything just goes south. It stops being about the mission and becomes purely about survival. You’re crouching in pitch black corners, technically blind because your night vision battery died, just desperately scrambling for any resource you can find. It’s absolute peak panic. Instead of feeling repetitive, those long, drawn out trials keep you hooked because the stakes feel so high when a chase starts out of nowhere. The sound design keeps that classic Outlast dread alive, but having the boys there makes the scares feel more like an adrenaline rush than just pure trauma. Playing solo is way too sweaty for me, but as a co-op horror experience, it’s easily the best thing I’ve played in a long long time.

Honestly, the Murkoff Corporation’s therapy is clearly working, because after an hour of this, my real life problems don't even seem that bad anymore.

Once you've completed a dozen trials or so It feels like you've seen everything the game has to offer. It's more shock-horror than the kind of horror that really scares me or makes me think. I don't know who this kind of thing really appeals to. Whether it's people who can't handle especially scary horror or just gateway horror fans.

Doesn't give much to do outside of gameplay. At least, I didn't find the minigames that interesting. Some of the outfits are cute but I arrived way too late to get them. For a $40 game I would have liked more customization when it came to your character; There's not much I could work with here.

When it comes to the trials itself, the enemy AI doesn't really feel that smart. The difficulty isn't that imposing, enemies are easy to evade, you're given too much time, especially with other players, and it doesn't even feel like the loadouts you have matter too much in the grand scheme of things. I do get that there's higher difficulties for this, but I can't play them yet, and I don't have the patience to unlock it. It feels like a big grind to unlock the things you do want.

Kind of feels like friendslop at best, which sucks. And I am sorely disappointed by the use of GenAI. I wanted better for this game.
Cute and fun game to play after a long day of work! Very wholesome atmospehre makes u feel like you're at home snuggling with your cat under the blanket on a rainy day. Strongly recommend!

while not exactly scary after you play it every day for several weeks like me, this is the only coop-horror game (that I've played) that is still fun even on your own, or with randoms, and gives more than enough motivation to keep coming back and playing. also the lore, characters and environments are very interesting and immersive

Outlast Trials Game Review
(Disclaimer - This is just my opinon and may be unpopular or disliked.)

(General)
1. This game is a First-Person, Psychological, Survival, Horror Game.
2. You are a person that is going through human trials of some sort hoping to one day be free.

(Positive)
1. The graphics are done well and the game looks and runs really well.
2. There is a lot of work put into your character, N.P.C.'s, enemies, maps, etc and has variety keeping it interesting. 
3. There is customization options for your character and room, which is nice and has variety.
4. The theme does stick true to horror in the maps, characters, gore and tension.
5. The map character stories and story is interesting. The lore has a lot of work put into it.
6. The gameplay is fun even though it is difficult.
7. You can play solo or with friends of which both are fun and do-able, but it is better with friends as it helps in game tasks to get done quicker.
8. The game is set before Outlast 1 and 2 and has characters and things mentioned in the other games to keep to the theme of Outlast even though it plays nothing like the others and is not as story driven.
9. It is a good take on a different twist on how Outlast could've been.

(Negative)
1. It feels more than a game based on the characters and things from Outlast rather than entirely cannon, etc.
2. It is better to play with at least 3 people including yourself, which is sometimes a negative for me if you wanna solo or just hang with one person.
3. The game is not nearly as scary or tense as Outlast 1 was and plays more like an Action game with horror elements. (It can still be tense though, but a different kind of tense.)
Overpriced friend slop that gets boring after a week or two but that its the most fun week or two you might ever have.

Over at least 200 assets use AI-generated slop. A lot of these are in catalogs from old events that can now be purchased with real money, so you're paying for something that nobody really worked on. Red Barrels failed to disclose this information during Early Access and during release until Valve greenlit the "AI Disclosure Policy" on Steam. I know these developers are not acting in good faith because this very information wasn't addressed in any other store, and they keep resorting to this unethical method to this day. I recently repurchased this game and some of the DLC for €1.49 on sale, not knowing of this, only to find out about it through Steam forums, which is entirely my fault. Now, there is a lot of good in it, and most of it is worked on by humans. The gameplay loop is extremely addictive and fun, the UI has charm to it, and the story and soundtrack are good, but if this company's excuse for using AI is to speed up the development process and make it easier for the artists, then take more time with development or hire a few more people. I'm pretty sure there are a lot of talented humans who are in need of a job. No company, regardless of it being indie, AA, or AAA, should get away with this, and it doesn't matter if most players won't ever notice these details because even so, these add personality to games, and there will always be people who will appreciate them, not to mention that we want everyone to keep their jobs. No more excuses and no more lies. I'm tired of being lied to and deceived by these greedy gaming companies. The irony of it all is that Outlast is a franchise about Murkoff being a corrupt and greedy capitalistic company, and Red Barrels proceeds to be corrupt and greedy.
This game has been a blast to play with friends. The map layouts and enemies will keep you on your toes. Overall, it's a great game and I would recommend trying it out.

If you're a masochist, if you're the type of person that enjoys being in pain with literally no end in sight, if you enjoy seeing your friends go through hell and back and wish more torture upon yourself and your friends, this is the game for you.

On an unrelated note, this game's great.

👍 Recommended
🕒 50.2 hours on record  

The Outlast Trials is a fantastic evolution of the Outlast series and one of the best co-op horror games available right now. With 50 hours on record, I can confidently say it delivers constant tension, great atmosphere, and a lot of replayability.

The game excels at creating a deeply disturbing and immersive experience. The sound design is excellent, the environments feel oppressive and unsettling, and the Murkoff facility genuinely makes you feel uneasy the entire time. While it does have some jump scares, the real strength lies in the constant pressure and paranoia it builds.

Where the game truly shines is in co-op. Playing with friends turns it from a scary horror game into an absolute chaotic comedy. The screaming, panic, betrayals, and clutch moments when someone gets grabbed make for some of the funniest and most memorable gaming sessions. The trials are well-designed and feel different enough to keep you coming back.

Progression is satisfying, with lots of trials, difficulties, and challenges to tackle. The game does suffer a bit from slow content updates and occasional matchmaking/balance issues at higher levels, but the core experience is strong enough to carry it.

If you enjoy horror games and have friends to play with, The Outlast Trials is a must-play. It’s tense, scary, and incredibly fun in a group. Even after 50 hours, I still look forward to jumping back in for more therapy sessions.

Highly recommended with friends. 🎥🩸😱

One of the best multiplayer horror games in the market, very heavily focused into a ghost-stealth playstyle so both stealth and horror fans will have a blast with it. Game is super fun solo but even more so with friends, all the prime targets feel very unique and so do the rigs, and the constant updates just make the game better and better. (But for the love of god, balance the invasion mode correctly)
the steam reviews were right, this is a VERY wholesome family friendly and chill game to play with your friends
this game was designed for reddit gore enjoyers and perverted sadists. also rlly fun in groups!

You think you’re just buying a co-op horror game, but that’s exactly what they want you to believe. Pay attention to how quickly you start trusting the instructions, following orders, sacrificing strangers to survive. 

The Murkoff trials aren’t fiction, they’re conditioning and if you finish the game smiling, congratulations it worked on you too.

it's like if DBD and Phasmo had a baby. Not really scary but the puzzles are fun to do with friends. I wouldn't play this game alone though.

The puzzles have semi-random spawns so the replayability is quite good. I was 100hrs in when a new friend joined and we still had fun together. Difficulty goes up exponentially with each level so newbies to experienced players all have a place.

This game is mainly PvE focused. Coop vs AI. If you're getting into this game for the PvP you're probably in the wrong place.

The Outlast Trials is a bloody good time. It's the good solo or co-op horror game available right now if you want something that actually scares you while letting you play with friends. Whether you're going in solo for that pure helpless terror or readying up for maximum solo chaos, it successfully captures that raw Outlast dread and puts it in a fresh, addictive package.

I'm a coward when it comes to jumpscares. I have a habit of hiding way too much in horror games, especially if there are stalker enemies. Despite that I've been having an absolute blast in the Outlast Trials, even if I've chucked my mouse a couple times from the Skinner Man. I've only played solo so I can't comment on how co-op feels, but I've never thought "Man I can really tell this was designed with co-op in mind".
Edit: I've now played with co-op and I won't lie it IS more fun. Some levels are really easy to get lost in, but playing co-op makes it feel a lot more manageable.

An addicting game. If you don't mind horror, dark humor and smutty content. Best to play with friends or make new friends in lobby! The different mini games in lobby help while waiting on friends to load up the game. Customizing your room and character make it just as fun for me and my friends.
This game is really fun. Its all the good of DBD with none of the bad. Very good with player QoL, and very fun with friends.

Honestly this is an extremely fun game. There’s so many trials to choose from you don’t really have to deal with repetitive gameplay like other games. There’s graphics are amazing, even on low end PCs. I use a discontinued Lenovo gaming laptop and can run it on the highest graphics with no gameplay issues. Do not play this if you have a weak heart or stomach. If you don’t have either of those, get it!

My therapist told me to try exposure therapy, then threw me into the trials. I have been beaten and molested by the homeless and deranged more times than I can count, and I think the lobotomy is working. 10/10 game.

this game is awesome, gives you a chilling and thrilling experience as you progressively learn every single aspect of this game. i wouldnt change anything on this game, worth buying and your time! 10/10

for years, i grew up playing horror games with my brother. him and i would play every horror game we could get our hands on together, and Outlast became one of the most unforgettable experiences we shared. we would stay awake until 6 in the morning, exhausted but too terrified to stop playing, trying to survive nightmare mode while laughing through the fear. those nights became memories wrapped in wistfulness the kind that ache because they can never truly be reached again no matter how badly you want to return to them.

years later, i found myself playing The Outlast Trials with THE Boy i loved deeply, alongside a very close friend. i had introduced them both to Outlast a long time ago, and they knew how much the series meant to me because of my brother. i used to joke that we would never recover from the game, remembering how badly me and my brother used to panic while playing the older titles. but somehow, history repeated itself in a different way.

we played for hours every single day. sometimes during the afternoon, sometimes late into the night, losing track of time while screaming, laughs that would hurt our stomachs, and the stress of not wanting to fail the trials over and over again, and somehow making memories that felt bigger than the game itself. i had never really played multiplayer games with anyone outside of my family before, so sharing that experience with people i cared about meant more to me than i can properly explain. there was something strangely comforting about it hearing everyone panic at the same time, trying to survive together, joking between moments of fear. it reminded me of me and my brother, but they were now all 3 of our memories. even the smallest moments stayed with me.

but now it has been a long while since the three of us last played together.

that is the painful thing about multiplayer games that people rarely talk about. they are never just games. they become tied to the people you experienced them with. everything in the game carrying memories inside of it. the people you choose to play with are people close enough to share pieces of your life with. and when those moments end, the game stays behind almost like a ghost of those memories.

The Outlast Trials became that for me.

whenever i look at the game on my library or go back and watch clips it feels less like remembering a game and more like remembering a period of my life that quietly disappeared. memories wrapped in wistfulness that can no longer reach back to where they once were. there is a strange sadness in knowing you can replay the game whenever you want, but you can never replay the exact feeling of those moments, those people, or that time in your life again.

and despite all of that, i still love The Outlast Trials deeply. i think it is one of the most fun horror multiplayer experiences out there, especially for people who already love the Outlast series. while it may not be quite as relentlessly terrifying as the two main games, it still captures the same intensity that makes you scream when something suddenly appears behind you. but what makes it special is how it turns horror into something shared. it creates memories that stay long after the matches are over.

for horror fans, especially longtime Outlast players, i could not recommend it enough. not just because it is scary, but because sometimes the best games become tied to the people you experienced them with and those memories end up haunting you far longer than the monsters ever could.

we never know when the time comes where we all stop playing or have finished the game completely, only afterwards when the silence begins to haunt the places our voices once lived…✃𓄧꒷꒦

I don't recommend this game to anyone who may suffer from epilepsy, purely because of the psychosis. Other than that, if you're okay with gore and heavy themes, this is a fine multiplayer game to play with your friends, though quite disturbing and gnarly at times.

That being said, the recent updates have made the game more glitchy and laggy, not sure if it's just my device so this is just my view since other games work fine for me.

its a pretty good game , not much story driven , but overall a nice experience and i can say i had a lot of fun playing this game , one thing i would like is them adding a concrete campaign with a good end like previous outlast games, either singleplayer or co op , otherwise , very solid game highly recommended especially to play with friends!

I WILL recommend this game to anyone willing to buy it. If if you’re looking for a multiplayer horror game, THIS IS THE GAME. It Doesn’t feel very repetitive with all the changes you can make to the trials including difficulties, modifiers, and limited time events. 

Most of the negative reviews on this game are very nitpicky and looking for any reason to degrade this game. they MAY have used AI for their game, but that only lets them spend money on other parts of their game including new content, bug fixes, etc.

For $40 it is 100% worth it in my book,but A LOT of the time this game is on sale for $11.99 which is crazy good deal

Absolutely in love with this game. It took me so long to actually play it. I've been hooked since I started though and am absolutely set on grinding through everything. The scare factor is there, especially as a noobie. At some point your mindset does shift into a more strategic one where you're trying to survive. However, I still scream and get scared almost every trial, especially playing alone. There's a lot to do for short term play and long term play. I definitely recommend this to anyone who enjoys the Outlast franchise and even for those who aren't familiar with it at all.

Its fun for a couple hours and the story is quite good i wont lie but it does get very boring towards the 5-8 hour mark even whilst playng with friends. its very repetitive and it becomes more like a chore than a game, i wouldn't reconmend it but i wouldn't not reconmend it, but if you were to get it, make sure its on sale.

The Outlast Trials somehow manages to be one of the funniest and most terrifying games I’ve ever played. The atmosphere hits this perfect balance of absolute dread and total chaos. Every trial feels like a nightmarish fever dream after I took a concerning amount of Benadryl and wandered onto a public subway. Nothing makes sense, everyone wants to kill you, and you’re stumbling through one horrifying situation after another wondering how things keep getting worse. Yet despite all of that, the game somehow creates some of the most hilarious moments imaginable. --- Anyways, I’m here to establish dominance at the arm wrestling table.

This is one of my favorite games now... I will kiss every member of red barrels on the mouth with tung <3

I haven't written a review in some time but this game just needs one. In my opinion the outlast trials is the BEST multiplayer horror expierience, it is a horror yes it does spook sometimes, it also is very replayable since new content gets regularly added, it is also really fun and i enjoyed every single minute i spent on the game, it has tons of lore and has enough content to keep you hooked but at the same time not too much so that you can understand the game after some time. PLAY THIS GAME!

highly recomend especially if you have freinds to play with but, even solo or randoms is fun, the game really evolve with your play time and understending. its starts as an horror game but as you understand the enemys and maaps the game become more like a huge chase while you complate OBJs (really simaler to DBD), and im sure as i get more time the game would evolve agin. the community is fun the lobby mini-games are fun (1v1 me in chese). tl;dr wierd uniqe game that the closest thing to compere is DBD but not really
Very fun game to play. Honest to God would rather play this game over that crap game DBD anyday. lmao
Great horror game with lots of missions and maps, great to play with a group of friends as well.
fun game to paly with Friends ,but at some point the game  is getting a little bit corny
the rubberbanding got so much worse after the recent update . i just want to goon to coyle in peace

Fun game! A super tense and nerve wracking experience that is best experienced with friends! All the trial environments are filled with creepy and disgusting decoration, i want to be that goose puppet, and the puzzles, while fairly cryptic and complicated at times, are super rewarding to solve! Highly recommend!
fun family night cozy game, recommended wholeheartedly
i really want to play this game, okay? it's just that i get scared super easily. i can tell its incredibly well made, so well made i couldnt finish the tutorial. :'D
This game is creepy, disgusting, and stressful as all hell to play with my friends. I love it.

at first this game was super duper laggy, but honestly i'm loving it. i also love the voice chat and how it gets quieter and sounds more like a radio the further away you are from people. super enjoyable
Consistently updated and always feeling like theres more to learn and to feel scared of in this game 1000000/10
I spend the entire time either ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ myself in a corner, or sprinting in circles screaming.

Genuinely a fun and still yet horrifying game, with brand new updates over the past year. I originally stopped playing for a year cause i had beat all the levels but i can still come back to new mobs and very difficult levels to go through, which is what i like in my opinion. I also personally love the chess, arm wrestle, tennis and the stroop test in the main lobby, I find it fun to play those with friends before hopping into the thrilling gameplay. Also really enjoy the mechanics and all the different perks you can bring in, overall a very difficult and challenging game that feels so satisfying when you and you friends complete. If there is something to complain about, it has to do with the mobs being to proactive and very difficult to escape in certain maps but other than that. A very solid and fun game :)

This game is very fun with friends and what not, has a decent horror vibe to it and can be scary at times, can customize your room and character which adds bonus points, and has good graphics as well as movement. I don't think this game is worth $40 but on discount I think this game is amazing.

Had so much fun with my friends in this game, had so many funny moments, and its just fun when you see your friend running accross a hall screaming his lungs out and then see that some Big naked guy is chasing him, i have so many clips from this game that i look at time to time and just laugh with my friends about it, 9/10 game with friends
It's so cool and underrated people say it's ♥♥♥♥♥♥ up but it's not that ♥♥♥♥♥♥ up

This game is incredibly fun, and its difficulty scaling makes it accessible for every caliber of gamer!
If you liked the previous Outlast titles and thought to yourself, "Man this would be cool if I could play it cooperatively," then this is absolutely the title for you!
Don't listen to the reviews comparing it to other games (except its previous iterations, of course). Outlast Trials simply carves a space for itself in the multiplayer/co-op gaming scene, and should absolutely be experienced by fans of co-op games and horror media alike!
This is the best game i've ever played no cap on god bro, i'm all about that sigma life baby.
this game is more of a speedrun, technical game then a horror game. it lacks All of the curated, scripted scares from Outlast 1 and 2, but gains a lot of fun party interaction
Fun, scary, silly, scary, creepy, jump scare. When I lag its a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ nightmare, its so bad. I also think I'm a victim of gang rape on this game.. Anyway 10/10 highly recommend!
I bought this game just for the chess in the lobby and it was 100% worth it.
Fun and spooky. Good to play with friends.
Amazing Game, very scary at times but also challenging too. This game is for physchopaths and people with strong stomaches but still fun!

this game brings tears to my eyes and white tears down my thighs.

100/10 get the game.

P.S I got Molested by a 8ft giant with his tiny ♥♥♥♥ game is very peak.

The whole family gets together every Friday to play this game even my seven year old sister!

It's really funny, cozy, and cute!

I highly recommend it, it’s one of the best family games out there!

The best horror franchise in my opinion. In this game we can finally abuse our enemies with bottles, bricks and rigs and not just hide or run. The game itself worth buying. Coyle is the best Prime Asset!!! Love him.
Kinda fun like you ir in a saw movie problem is the game is a bit confusing on what you need to do but not hard to learn i gues might be more fun with people rather then solo
dont play it if youre scared of like other games like the last of us but if you can play it play it its really good with friends ofcourse

This game will be one of the top scary games you play.

 Peak game The devs are not money hunger they truly have passion for the future of The Outlast Trials.

Runs smooth very optimised on a low end PC and beautiful graphics on high end.
If you want to lose your mental losing connection after 45 mins of trying to figure a map out just to get nothing from it because you lose connection. Play this game! happened to me 5 games in a row

You are guaranteed to either puke a little or ♥♥♥♥ your pants hard enough to cause a nuclear war in your area. The commitment to intense and grotesque horror is really what stands out the most to me. So much detail in each environment and chases/hiding from monsters gives me that very real feeling that I am going to die if I don't run.

What a beautiful horror game!

ehh do we get a dating event one day
eiiiiiiiiiither way great game love it nothings ever felt quite as good it changed me
fun horror game with friends and is actually scary 10/10

Really good game. Creators definitely have something physiologically wrong with them but that's okay! The entire aspect of this game is amazing, the idea is brilliant and the maps are very well made. 

The attention to detail is amazing, even though my PC Specs aren't great, I can still run this game pretty well and still enjoy myself with my mates. 

If your into intense horror and love watching people getting chopped into 1000 pieces this is definitely your game!

Outlast trials. What can I say. Well I say the team of people who made this, is 100% psychopaths, but in a good way.

I have played this game over 50 hours on epic games, and then decided to buy it on steam as Epic sucks. (Yes, we can all agree).

Me and my mate, Isaac, have played this game and have more than just a giggle but an absolute turtle neck hiding in our boxers. This game has both the quality of entertainment and absolute horror. So if you enjoy new trials, and constant challenges, I highly suggest this game.
This game is absolutely awesome. The visuals are sick, the concepts for the Primes are awesome, and overall when you play with your friends its many hours worth of laughs.
